key_findings.note_1_organization: Entity franchises all Hilton brands in US/territories/Thailand for post-Oct-2007 contracts. Licenses IP royalty-free from Hilton affiliate.
key_findings.note_2_revenue_recognition: Royalty fees = variable (% GRR), recognized as services provided. Sales/COO fees = fixed, recognized over contract term. Termination fees recognized when received.
key_findings.note_3_acquisition: May 2024: acquired Graduate brand franchise contracts (~$85M intangible, 15-year amortization). Accounted as asset acquisition.
key_findings.note_4_franchise_contracts: Net carrying value $392.7M (2024). Contract acquisition costs $306.2M + acquired intangibles $82.0M + costs to obtain $4.5M. Amortization: $16.1M (2024) as reduction to royalty fees.
key_findings.note_6_taxes: Disregarded entity for US tax purposes — all taxable income flows to Hilton. Only territorial/foreign taxes apply.
key_findings.note_7_commitments: ALL assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral for Hilton's debt (maturing 2025-2033). No guarantee liability recorded.
key_findings.note_8_related_party: Intercompany balances from Hilton's centralized cash management (not controlled by Company). $1.5B settled in 2024 with simultaneous $1.5B distribution. Operator agreement with affiliate — $0 compensation.
key_findings.note_9_subsequent_events: Evaluated through March 18, 2025 (document states 2024, likely typo).

critical_observation: All Company assets pledged as collateral for Hilton parent's debt. Entity is a disregarded pass-through for tax purposes. Not a standalone going concern — entirely dependent on Hilton Worldwide structure.
